story.iWant: pre-built mock implementations of framework interfaces
story.soThat: I can quickly write unit tests without creating mocks manually

subtasks.subtask: Create `sdk/testutils/` package following established SDK patterns
Set up package structure with `mocks.go`, `builders.go`, `fixtures.go`
Add package documentation and usage overview
Create mock interface compliance tests
Create MockEndorService implementing EndorServiceInterface with testify/mock
Create MockEndorHybridService implementing EndorHybridServiceInterface
Create MockRepository implementing RepositoryInterface[T] with generic support
Create MockConfigProvider implementing ConfigProviderInterface
Create MockEndorContext implementing EndorContextInterface[T]
Add behavior configuration methods for all mocks

task.title: Create test data builders and fixtures

subtasks.subtask: Create service builders: `NewTestEndorService()`, `NewTestHybridService()`
Create repository builders with realistic test data
Create configuration builders for different test environments
Create context builders with test session and payload data
Add performance simulation capabilities for latency testing
Create setup helpers: `SetupTestService()`, `SetupTestEnvironment()`
Create assertion helpers for common testing scenarios
Create test lifecycle helpers: setup, teardown, cleanup
Add utilities for testing service composition patterns
Create unit testing examples for each interface type
Create service composition testing examples
Create error scenario testing examples
Add performance testing example patterns
Document testing best practices and common patterns
Create in-memory repository implementations for integration tests
Add test database utilities for MongoDB integration testing
Create realistic test data sets for different service types
Add performance benchmarking utilities
Create helpers for testing service lifecycle management

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `sdk/testutils` package provides ready-to-use mock implementations for all framework interfaces
MockEndorService, MockEndorHybridService, MockRepository, MockConfigProvider, and MockEndorContext support behavior configuration (return values, errors, call tracking)
Helper functions exist for common test scenarios (setup service, create test data, test schemas, realistic test fixtures)
Examples demonstrate testing patterns for each service type with complete usage documentation
Performance mocks simulate realistic latency and load for integration testing scenarios
All mock implementations satisfy their respective interface contracts with validation tests

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: Test utility package with pre-built mocks for all framework interfaces. MockEndorService, MockEndorHybridService, MockRepository implementations with behavior configuration support and helper functions for common test scenarios.

artifact.title: Epic Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: MockEndorService, MockEndorHybridService, MockRepository implementations available with behavior configuration support. Test data builders create realistic test scenarios. Helper functions: NewTestEndorService(), MockWithBehavior().

artifact.title: Refactored Architecture Document
artifact.section: Decision 5: Testing Strategy and Test Organization
artifact.snippet: Unit tests use interface-based mocking for fast execution. Integration tests separated with build tags. Test utilities package provides mock implementations and test data builders.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: FR6: Framework provides test utility package with pre-built mocks for common interfaces. FR7: Developers can test service composition patterns in isolation. FR8: Integration tests can use in-memory or test database implementations.

artifact.reason: Core service interface that MockEndorService must implement
Hybrid service interface that MockEndorHybridService must implement
Repository interface pattern for MockRepository implementation
Configuration interface that MockConfigProvider must implement
Context interface that MockEndorContext must implement
Interface compliance testing pattern to follow for mock validation
Existing testing patterns to modernize with interface-based mocks
Service action creation pattern for test builders
Repository initialization pattern for mock repository builders

go.reason: Already available - core testing framework with mock support
HTTP framework for context and handler testing
MongoDB client for repository testing patterns
Metrics framework for performance testing utilities

constraints.constraint: All mock implementations must satisfy their respective interface contracts using compile-time verification: var _ Interface = (*MockImpl)(nil)
Use testify/mock framework for behavior verification and call tracking. Mocks must support On(), Return(), AssertExpectations() patterns
Follow established SDK package patterns: mocks.go for mock implementations, builders.go for test data builders, fixtures.go for test fixtures
Performance mocks must simulate realistic latency (50ms-500ms) for integration testing scenarios without requiring actual external dependencies
MockRepository must support generic types following Go 1.21+ generics patterns for type-safe repository testing
Unit tests using test utilities must execute without MongoDB, external APIs, or filesystem dependencies
Test utilities must support both existing testing patterns and new interface-based patterns during migration
All public mock types and helper functions must have comprehensive GoDoc with usage examples

interface.name: EndorServiceInterface
interface.signature: type EndorServiceInterface interface { GetResource() string GetDescription() string GetMethods() map[string]EndorServiceAction GetPriority() *int GetVersion() string Validate() error }

interface.name: EndorHybridServiceInterface
interface.signature: type EndorHybridServiceInterface interface { GetResource() string GetResourceDescription() string GetPriority() *int WithCategories(categories []EndorHybridServiceCategory) EndorHybridServiceInterface WithActions(fn func(getSchema func() RootSchema) map[string]EndorServiceAction) EndorHybridServiceInterface ToEndorService(metadataSchema Schema) EndorServiceInterface Validate() error }

interface.name: ConfigProviderInterface
interface.signature: type ConfigProviderInterface interface { GetServerPort() string GetDocumentDBUri() string IsHybridResourcesEnabled() bool IsDynamicResourcesEnabled() bool GetDynamicResourceDocumentDBName() string Reload() error Validate() error }

interface.name: EndorContextInterface[T any]
RepositoryPattern

tests.standards: Go testing with testify/mock framework for behavior verification. Interface compliance testing using var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern. Unit tests execute in <100ms without external dependencies. Build tags separate unit tests from integration tests. Comprehensive GoDoc with usage examples for all public test utilities.
tests.locations: sdk/testutils/ for test utility package, test/ directory for existing tests, sdk/interfaces/ for interface compliance tests

ideas.test: Verify MockEndorService, MockEndorHybridService, MockRepository, MockConfigProvider, and MockEndorContext satisfy their respective interfaces using compile-time checks
Test that mocks support On().Return() patterns for return values, error injection, and call tracking with AssertExpectations()
Validate NewTestEndorService(), NewTestHybridService(), test configuration builders, and fixture generation for realistic test scenarios
Test all documented examples compile and execute correctly, demonstrating patterns for testing each interface type
Verify performance mocks simulate realistic latency (50ms-500ms) for integration testing without external dependencies
Ensure mock implementations work correctly with existing framework patterns and can replace concrete implementations in tests
